what_it_does: Defines a JSON substrate for multi-page sequence composition: narrative roles, role order, pacing rules, growth policies, content-to-role mapping, fusion tiers, reading axes, and a root-first generation ladder.
observations: Declares seven narrative roles with descriptions, archetype affinities, evidence notes, and dial envelopes.
Represents sequence structure as ordered role runs with elastic page counts and named growth policies.
Includes rules for bookends, pacing, title chaining, motif recurrence, dial choreography, signal economy, dividers, spreads, and progressive builds.
Maps content unit types such as claim, demo, stat, timeline, person, terms, and contact to narrative roles.
Models scale tiers from tagline through one-pager, teaser, short, and full, including fusion and extract strategies.
Defines reading_axes with descent, traverse, walks, fields, and measure as ways to read or navigate the story lattice.
Specifies a generation ladder from seed brief through root, roles, titles, frames, slots, voice, and tokens.
standouts: The file treats page order as narrative relation rather than page rank.
Fusion, extraction, nesting, and elastic runs let the same story exist at different page counts.
Reading_axes generalizes sequencing into traversals, walks, gradients, arrows, and conserved story measure.
conventions: JSON object organized as named registries with explanatory _note, _status, and evidence fields.
Uses compact role identifiers such as open, argue, show, prove, plan, people, and close.
Evidence is recorded inline as deck slide references, corpus-direct notes, and dated harvest annotations.
Dial envelopes use warmth ranges, register arrays, and density labels.
Some fields are operational while others are interpretive prose or falsifiable prediction notes.
concerns: The file mixes machine-actionable data with long prose explanations, which may make strict consumption harder.
Several statuses and evidence notes depend on external harvest material not included in this file.
Some values are nullable or freeform where a resolver may need stricter shapes.
notes: Brief this as the sequence grammar for composing and reading multi-page artifacts across roles, tiers, pacing, and traversal paths.
questions: What schema validates the prose-heavy fields, nullable fusion value, and mixed scalar or range page counts?
How should a consumer choose between fuse and extract strategies for a one-pager from this file alone?
Which fields are intended as resolver inputs versus documentation-only annotations?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it describes how pages go together across roles, arcs, pacing, reading axes, and generation stages.
apparent_themes: fusion and extraction across page counts
intention_signals: It seems intended to preserve observed sequence mechanics as a reusable, file-local grammar for planning, checking, and generating multi-page design artifacts.
novelty: It frames deck sequencing as a conserved story lattice with legal walks, role fusion, dial choreography, and generation stages rather than only a slide order.
contribution: Contributes the sequence layer: role definitions, ordered role runs, growth policies, tier fusion and extraction behavior, reading paths, pacing rules, and generation stages that a fused system would retain as the multi-page composition substrate.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON design substrate defining multi-page sequence mechanics through narrative roles, ordered runs, pacing rules, content-role mapping, scale tiers, reading axes, and a generation ladder.
intention_for_embedding: It was made to turn observed deck/story mechanics into an operational grammar for composing, scaling, and reading page sequences.
